title: Rhythm: high-frequency words paragraph
description: Rhythm exercise - high-frequency words flowing as natural prose

tips.text: No new keys on this page. We settle into a tempo and let plain words flow, the way good prose does on a quiet morning.
Keep your fingers home and your eyes forward. Repeat each word at one even pace, no rushing, no stalling. The rhythm is the lesson.

tips.title: Find the flow
tips.text: Hold a steady beat between keystrokes, like a tune you can keep without thinking. Even and calm beats fast and broken. Even keystrokes read like clean copy.

tips.title: Rhythm: work
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: work work work. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.title: Rhythm: years
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: years years years. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.title: Rhythm: change
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: change change change. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.text: Prose has a rhythm of its own, and your hands can learn to ride it. Find the even pace here and a full page stops feeling like work and starts to read like flow.
Worked through every step? Open the trainer below and make one full run on the lesson text. That run saves your progress and unlocks the next lesson.

character_tips.anna: Real writing flows. Match the tempo of speech, not single keystrokes.
character_tips.maxim: Prose has its own rhythm - find it and ride it.
character_tips.knopych: FLOW STATE! 🌊 Don't break the wave!
character_tips.klavochka: Like reading aloud, my dear - smooth and steady 📖
